description: The Kerryonians were an Irish-American street gang based in the Five Points area of New York City, active from the 1820s to the 1830s. Founded by recent immigrants from County Kerry, Ireland, they were among the earliest criminal gangs in the city, known for targeting British-descended New Yorkers and disrupting a performance by actor William Charles Macready in 1849. The gang was eventually absorbed into larger Five Points gangs such as the Dead Rabbits, Roach Guards, and Chichesters.
